rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues may include (but not limited to): No petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report of status of administration has been filed; and no final inventory and appraisal has been filed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Please review the Court's 8th Amended General Order which took effect on 12/07/2020 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN BARRY BALDWIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible due to the recent stay at home order issued by the mayor. Please review the 8th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES (to be addressed at hearing): 1) Petitioner alleges Decedent's Will is lost. Probate Code 6124 If the testator's will was last in the testator's possession, the testator was competent until death, and neither the will nor a duplicate original of the will can be found after the testator's death, it is presumed that the testator destroyed the will with intent to revoke it. This presumption is a presumption affecting the burden of producing evidence. 2) Whether the actions taken to locate Cynthia Mansfield are sufficient.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NORMAN M. STEWART SR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible due to the recent stay at home order issued by the mayor. Please review the 8th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES (to be addressed at hearing): 1) Petitioner checked Petition for Probate of Lost Will and for Letters Testamentary. Petitioner also checked Letters of Administration and requested Petitioner be appointed administrator. 2) Petitioner alleges the Lost Will was a pour over will to the Stewart Living Trust which is also lost. Petitioner says the presumption of California Probate Code 6124 do not apply as Decedent did not intended to revoke or destroy the Stewart Living Trust. Unclear if there are probate assets. The Declaration of real property shows the only property as 1734 Lyon Street the same property that was Quitclaimed into the Trust on 02/05/2004.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GARY CASELLA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/01/2021 (no earlier date available). 1) Item 5b of the petition must be completed. 2) Item 8 (Attachment 8) does not list Evelyn Casella's address. The proof of service does not show notice was given to her as named executor, although her nomination was purportedly revoked by the divorce. Probate Code section 8110(b). 3) Item 8 (Attachment 8) must clearly identify the beneficiaries of the devisee trust, as the proposed personal representative and trustee are the same person. LRSF 14.26(D)(2). 4) Notice of the amendment must be given on a mandatory Judicial Council form DE-121. Also, notice may not be mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. LRSF 14.21(A). 5) No proof of publication of notice of the amendment has been filed. CRC 7.53(a). 6) For petitions set for hearing on or after 01/04/2021, a confidential DE-147S form with a cover sheet must be conventionally filed (may not be e-filed or attached to a DE-147 form).
